message:
refactor(oracle): convert LoserDrainViolation from struct to enum with three variants

The original LoserDrainViolation was a flat struct that could only represent
the "undrained losers at race completion" case. The oracle can now observe
two additional impossible-instrumentation scenarios that the old type could
not express:

- ActiveRaceNotCompleted: a race was started but never reached completion
  by the time the oracle performed its check, suggesting either a hung race
  or missing instrumentation.
- UnknownRaceCompletion: a race completion event arrived without a matching
  prior start, indicating orphan instrumentation or a double-complete.

The existing UndrainedLosers variant preserves the original semantics
(race completed before all losers drained). The Display impl and all
downstream test assertions are updated to pattern-match on the new enum
shape rather than accessing struct fields directly.

This makes the oracle's violation surface richer and more precise, enabling
better diagnostics for cancel-correctness violations in both native and
browser-targeted race combinators.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
